Text[0]: You still here? Like I said, retrieving that data takes time.
Text[1]: So why am I hanging around out here like a bad smell, you ask? I just figured you'd miss this handsome mug too much if I stayed cooped up inside.

Text[0]: If you've any spare soul shards weighing down your pack, I'll be happy to take them off your hands. You'll be compensated, of course.
Keen to learn more about the isle?
Can't leave the ship unattended in its current state. I'll be standin' guard while ye help Ketenramm search the area.
The lookouts'll keep watch over the ship while I'm at the base camp. If anythin' goes awry, they'll soon get word to me via linkpearl.
Gettin' marooned is naught but a matter of course in this line of work. When ye get back to Tuliyollal, ye can tell the messenger we're doin' just fine.
Oh, it's you. Anything to report?
Confounded by a shifting mirage, only to find yourselves trapped when you did make landfall... Clearly Shades' Triangle deserves its fearsome reputation!
Text[1]: Still, I'm glad to hear the crew is safe. If there's any news, they'll get word to me via one of the relay vessels─you're free to do as you please in the meantime.

Text[0]: This seal on my knowledge is proving rather onerous. If I can only speak of that which you have seen firsthand, it almost makes sense for me to simply follow you everywhere you go...
When it comes to martial techniques and scholarship, people are quick to assume the new outclasses the old. On the contrary, I believe ancient knowledge still has its applications.
The Forked Tower has certainly piqued my curiosity. Much like the other ruins, it must have been built with a purpose...

Save for the second Forked Tower, I haven't the faintest clue what lies on the north horn─a state of ignorance no doubt caused by that vexatious seal. For the time being we shall have to wait for Ketenramm and his crew to make headway.
In a place like this, flexibility is key to success. Let's discuss our next move at the base camp.
With the crew's combined expertise, we're ready for almost anything the isle throws at us. An' with the secretary keepin' a record, I reckon our endeavors'll make for entertainin' readin'.
Text[1]: For a blessin', we've had no casualties to speak of so far, but we'd best stay vigilant.

Text[0]: A great pronged tower, shiftin' in an' out of view like some trick of the light... If yer intent on investigatin', ye'd best be well-prepared.

We're lookin' for a route through to the north of the isle, but with an expeditionary force this large, the logistics aren't always straightforward. At any rate, I'll get word to ye once we've got a new base camp established.
You sense an eastbound flow of aetherial energy. Riding this current will provide passage to the phantom village.
You sense a westbound flow of aetherial energy. Riding this current will provide passage to Tuliyollal.
The deplorable lack o' drink in this town's impactin' me mental faculties─I'm plagued with visions o' floatin' rum bottles.
I tell ye, if I were even 'alf as skilled as Gerolt, I'd 'ave damn sure figured out a way to stay out of debt...
The <Emphasis>Queen Lhaminn</Emphasis> takes her name from the Songstress of Ul'dah, whose ballads once captivated the whole of Eorzea.
Text[1]: We mustn't allow our safety standards to slip, lest we tarnish her honor along with our own.

Text[0]: The moment I laid eyes on our uniforms, I knew they were designed by a true professional.
Text[1]: Fabric suited to the garment's purpose! Seams as straight as arrows, with nary a thread out of place! Every ilm bespeaks a deep respect for those who guard against danger.
Text[2]: That said, someone really needs to pull poor T'zanza out of that “all work, no play” mindset. Maybe she'll loosen up if I offer to show her around...

Text[0]: So much of my life was spent cooped up in a workshop... When it comes to recreation, I fear I'm woefully out of my depth.
Text[1]: I considered the cannon ride, but Haermaga seemed to think it was too soon for me to go swimming again. I suppose he's right.
Text[2]: Yes, I think I'll be satisfied simply bearing witness to the merriment. The memory of this day shall energize me during even the most complex commissions!

Text[0]: My sister's been following me like a shadow all day long. “Someone's got to make sure you get your work done,” she said! Can you believe it?
Text[1]: Admittedly my adventuring duties <Emphasis>have</Emphasis> kept me rather busy of late... Now that my shift is over, I see no reason not to spend some proper time with her.

Text[0]: I've heard Fhul's speech so many times, I can recite it all by myself! Does that mean I can join the guard, too?
With my shift thus concluded, I intend to swim, dance, and frolic to my heart's content─starting with the cannon ride!
Text[1]: It was torture, I tell you, being forced to stand here and watch everyone else have the time of their lives. Now <Emphasis>they</Emphasis> shall watch as I soar like a bird!

Text[0]: I'm glad to be out of that freezing seawater! Now to sit back and relax...
Text[1]: ...For gods' sakes, is that Beaudefoin lining up for the cannon ride? I'm going to need a stiff drink.

Text[0]: You won't want to miss this year's Moonfire Faire. Chart a course for Costa del Sol before the <Emphasis>Queen Lhaminn</Emphasis> sets sail for good!
Even the journey to the encampment is fraught with dangers─hence my insistence on traveling in groups for protection!
Space on board the ship was scarce, so I only brought the bare minimum of tools and equipment. Everything else I can simply craft out here in the field as the need arises!
When exploring strange lands, brains trump brawn any day of the week. Use your head and don't go charging heedlessly into the fray!
Apparently, there's a magical barrier surrounding the island─not that you notice it while exploring. Still, it might be worth getting some practice in before you head out.
We have plenty of provisions for the interim, but for an extended stay we'll need to become self-sufficient. I'm currently examining the soil to see what seeds might be viable for cultivation.
For all we know, the isle may be harboring diseases unknown to medical science. If you find yourself suffering from any untoward symptoms, let me know immediately!
I moved here with my dad, but I used to play Triple Triad with my friends all the time back in Tuliyollal... Do you wanna play a match, <If(PlayerParameter(4))>miss<Else/>mister</If>?
Haha, I won! I'm the greatest!
Text[1]: But you weren't bad either, <If(PlayerParameter(4))>miss<Else/>mister</If>! You even had me worried there... I'll think up even better strategies, so come back and play with me any time.

Text[0]: I lost!? You're amazing, <If(PlayerParameter(4))>miss<Else/>mister</If>... I was sure I'd win!
Text[1]: But I won't give up here─I'll practice hard so I can beat all my friends when I visit Tuliyollal again!

Text[0]: A draw, huh? I guess that just means we'll have to play again sometime. Thanks for the match, <If(PlayerParameter(4))>miss<Else/>mister</If>!
Our plots have been tilled and we've already started planting. With everyone's help, I'm sure we'll make a great gainery!
When I moved to Tuliyollal, I had to leave behind my wivre...
Text[1]: I was a little nervous that I'd lost my knack for taming, but I'm glad everything worked out. My Doppro roots aren't so easily shaken after all!

Text[0]: Thank you for coming with me to convince Mireel Ja. I have higher hopes than ever!
Text[1]: Now that we have more manpower, I'll have to think up what else can be done for the gainery...

Text[0]: You'd think maintaining our lights would be a simple task, but in truth our magical practitioners have to constantly adjust intensity, wavelength, heat, and all sorts of other things according to Anggir's readings.
Text[1]: And the readings change day by day! Really makes you appreciate the technical artistry we're working with.

Text[0]: Thanks for your help with the banquet. I got to speak properly with Hunmu Rruk afterwards, and we have plans to commence trade of our vegetables with Iq Br'aax.
Text[1]: It's all thanks to everyone who's worked so hard to bring change to this forest. I'm so glad to have met you and Anggir!

Text[0]: <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>, nice to see you! Gok Golma's still flourishing, thanks to everyone's efforts.
Text[1]: Now that we're a part of the Turali Agricultural Association, we've begun proper trade with outside communities. Which is why we need more help than ever around the gainery. If you've a moment to spare, feel free to lend us a hand!

Text[0]: I won't deny─I was a little nervous in coming here at first. But meeting you, Ruvoll Ja, and Kageel Ja has reassured me. I'm lucky to have made such happy acquaintances.
I had heard that Doppro have a knack for taming wivres, but that reputation undersells the extent of their talents.
Text[1]: Their reliance on building trust, as opposed to resorting to dominance, is apparent at a glance. It is little wonder that our branchbearers seem quite at home here.

Text[0]: I'm impressed by the Hoobigo patrol's eagerness to drive off errant beasts and assist in the gainery's labor-intensive needs.
Text[1]: I can hardly carry anything heavier than research texts, so I often find myself relying upon their kindness. I must think of a suitable way to show my thanks...

Text[0]: You may leave data collection to me. The Boonewa practitioners and I will ensure that Gok Golma prospers under the new lights, no matter the conditions.
I don't often pay much mind to what I eat, but tiika selvaas is truly remarkable.
Text[1]: Not only in taste but in the weight it carries for all who live in Yak T'el. When I think that I have played even a small part in its conception, I feel deeply humbled.

Text[0]: Good to see you, <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>. As you can see, Gok Golma is still thriving.
Text[1]: My study of selective breeding proceeds apace as well. We may need to expand our beds to facilitate further growth...but for that, I must first consult with the Autarch.

Text[0]: Growing anything but najool in this forest was unthinkable before. I can still barely believe that I've traded the grove for a gainery.
Text[1]: In our tongue, “Gok Golma” means “eternal color.” I named it with the hope that this place one day sees such a varied and bountiful harvest...

Text[0]: Taming branchbearers to tend our crops was a daring idea...not to mention wholesome, when you think of the harmony it represents.
The Hoobigo patrol has also been assisting with our najool. We've been so shorthanded recently that the extra help is invaluable.
Without Zereel Ja's ingenuity and cooperation, our lights would be nothing but a fanciful idea.
Text[1]: I think he truly believes in this gainery. If there's anything he might do to help, you shouldn't hesitate to speak with him.

Text[0]: Tiika selvaas is the manifestation of all our dreams. Not to mention the tastiest thing I've ever eaten.
Text[1]: I'm sure this accomplishment has whetted everyone's appetite for new endeavors. I hope to do everything I can for Gok Golma going forward.

Text[0]: Thank you for all your help around the gainery, <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>. Just look how far we've come.
Text[1]: I hope you'll continue to help our “eternal color” retain its vibrancy as we seek to extend Gok Golma's reach to communities far and wide.

Text[0]: I thought I'd teach our little branchbearers to bear a rider as well─that way, they can tend to most anything in these forests.
Text[1]: You're no stranger to riding, are you? If you ever find yourself in need of a steady branchbearer, you have only to ask.

Text[0]: The yields from our gainery may have stabilized, but our dreams for Gok Golma grow ever greater.
Text[1]: Our branchbearers stand ready to take you to any corner of the forest that needs tending, so don't hesitate to call.

Text[0]: Welcome, <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>. Thank you for your help around Gok Golma.
Text[1]: We trade in both foreign and local currencies, so feel free to peruse our wares.

Text[0]: Gok Golma continues to prosper, thanks to your efforts.
Text[1]: We have more wares now than ever. Please, have a look.

Text[0]: I'm a mender by trade─originally tasked with repairing Hoobigo weaponry, but these days I mostly see gardening equipment.
Text[1]: Having said that, if you need anything at all mended, I can see to it straightaway.

Text[0]: Lately, I've been commissioned by the Brethren of Br'aax, when they stop by for a bite to eat.
Text[1]: Being handed their precious weaponry with such trust...it makes me want to repay them in kind, by restoring their weapons to pristine condition!

Text[0]: Just as Ruvoll Ja promised, we've got something other than najool to chew on. Now it's just a matter of earning our keep.
You can leave the protection of Gok Golma to us Hoobigo. We may not be able to use magicks like our Boonewa brethren, but no one can beat us for physical strength.
Mamook really welcomed in our Xbr'aal neighbors! Now my friend from Tuliyollal can visit in comfort, too.
Gok Golma is the very image of peace. The other day, I helped with the harvest, and it was a pleasure to handle so many bright and various crops.
I-Is it really all right to take these? I never thought I'd see the day when such beautiful vegetables could be had at market...
The glow of najool never gave me a second thought, but all these different crops look positively elegant. And their flavor is peerless, too!
What shall I take home today... These nutritious vegetables have given my scales a new luster, if you'll believe it!
That gentleman Bol Tst'ei taught me a recipe I can easily make at home. Now all I need are ingredients.
Text[1]: My partner is delighted that meat from Iq Br'aax can be had at market now. It makes me all the more excited to cook, to discover new ways to impress!

Text[0]: These ingredients are an inspiration. Makes me want to invent another dish for the ages.
I enjoy cooking down here. You can't get glowing vegetables anywhere but Gok Golma, after all.
In the Mamool Ja tongue, “tiika selvaas” means “weight of the forest.” Kageel Ja named it for me.
Text[1]: As he described it, this forest was once weighed down by the meteorites' influence─but now that very weight has become a boon.
Text[2]: A boon from which I plan to partake today. With gratitude in my heart.

Text[0]: The Moonfire Faire is in full swing here on the <Emphasis>Queen Lhaminn</Emphasis>. If you're curious, ask Ethelia to tell you more about this year's festival!
I wonder how my friends in Tuliyollal are doing... We promised to play Triple Triad the next time I visit, so until then I'll practice hard and collect loads of rare cards!
Thanks to your offering, this little one will hardly leave my side!
Navigatin's a seafarin' skill, but I'm happy to muck in an' help out on land with the logistics. As for the actual explorin', I'll leave that to ye!

Ketenramm might be shiftin' to the next phase of the expedition, but there's still plenty to do here─meteorological charts to study, provisions to source...the list goes on. Suffice it to say, I'll be keepin' busy until the expedition resumes.
